text: Tell me about yourself.
Walk me through your background and what led you to software engineering.
Tell me about your most recent role and what you worked on.
Why are you looking for a new role, and what are you looking for in your next position?
What makes you a strong fit for this role?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of a project from start to finish. What was your role and what obstacles did you have to overcome?
Describe a time you had to make an important decision with incomplete or ambiguous information. How did you approach it?
Give an example of a time you stepped up to take ownership of something outside your defined responsibilities. What drove you to do it?
Tell me about a time you drove a project or initiative forward when there was no clear owner or direction. What did you do?
Describe a situation where you influenced a decision or outcome without having direct authority over the people involved.
Describe a time you disagreed with your manager or a senior colleague on an approach. How did you handle it?
Tell me about a time you pushed back on a decision you thought was wrong. What happened and what was the outcome?
Give an example of when you received critical feedback you initially disagreed with. How did you respond?
Describe a time you had to work with someone whose working style or priorities were very different from yours. How did you make it work?
Tell me about a time there was tension between moving fast and doing something the right way. How did you navigate that trade-off?
Tell me about a time you failed to meet a deadline or expectation. What happened and how did you respond?
Describe a mistake you made that had real consequences for your team or users. How did you handle it?
Tell me about a project or initiative that didn't go as planned. What went wrong and what did you learn?
Give an example of a time your assumptions about a problem turned out to be wrong. How did you discover that and what did you do?
Describe a time you had to deliver bad news to a stakeholder or your team. How did you approach that conversation?
Describe a time you worked closely with product, design, or another non-engineering team to ship something. How did you handle differing priorities?
Tell me about a time you helped a teammate who was struggling or blocked. What did you do and what was the outcome?
Give an example of when you had to explain a complex technical concept to someone without a technical background. How did you make it clear?
Describe a time you had to rely on someone else to get your work done and they weren't delivering. How did you handle it?
Tell me about a time you had to onboard onto an unfamiliar codebase or domain quickly and still deliver. How did you ramp up?
Tell me about the project you are most proud of. What was your specific contribution and why does it matter?
Describe a time you identified a problem or opportunity that others had missed. What did you do about it?
Give an example of a time you improved a process, workflow, or system that made a measurable difference for your team.
Tell me about a time you had to deliver results under significant constraints — tight deadline, limited resources, or unclear requirements.
Describe a time you had to prioritize ruthlessly between multiple important things. How did you decide what to focus on?
Tell me about yourself and why you're interested in this internship.
What's a project from school or a side project you're most excited about? Walk me through what you built and why.
Tell me about a time you took initiative on a class project or team assignment when no one else stepped up. What did you do?
Describe a time you had to collaborate with a group on a project where not everyone was contributing equally. How did you handle it?
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate or classmate on how to approach a problem. How did you resolve it?
Describe a time you made a mistake on a project or assignment. What did you learn from it?
Tell me about something you built or accomplished that you're proud of. What was the impact and what did it teach you?
Tell me about a time you had to learn a new tool, language, or framework quickly to get something done. How did you approach it?
Describe a situation where you had too much to do and not enough time. How did you decide what to work on first?
What's a skill or concept you've worked hardest to improve, and how did you go about getting better at it?
Walk me through your career trajectory. What's driven your most significant professional decisions?
Tell me about a time you drove a significant org-level or cross-team initiative. How did you get buy-in and keep things moving?
Describe a time you had to make a high-stakes architectural or strategic decision that affected the entire engineering team or product. How did you approach it?
Tell me about a time you had to navigate a serious disagreement between senior stakeholders or engineering leaders. What was your role and how did it resolve?
Describe a major project or initiative you led that failed or significantly underdelivered. What went wrong and how did you lead the team through it?
Tell me about a time you mentored or grew an engineer on your team. What was your approach and what was the outcome?
What's the highest-impact thing you've built or shipped in your career? How did you measure and communicate that impact?
Describe a time you changed how your team or organization works in a meaningful way — process, culture, or tooling. What drove you to do it?
Tell me about a time you had to influence a decision at the executive or director level without having formal authority.
Describe a time you had to tell a team or leadership that a project should be cancelled or fundamentally changed. How did you make that case?
Design a URL shortener like bit.ly. Walk me through your architecture, data model, and the trade-offs you considered.
How would you design a rate limiter for a high-traffic public API? What algorithms and data structures would you use?
Design a notification service that delivers push, email, and SMS at scale. What are the main bottlenecks and how do you handle them?
How would you design a distributed key-value cache? Walk through consistency guarantees, eviction policies, and fault tolerance.
Design a background job queue for processing async tasks. How do you handle retries, ordering, and poison messages?
How would you design a scalable news feed (like Twitter or Instagram)? Focus on the fan-out model, storage, and read latency.
Describe a production bug or incident you were involved in that was particularly hard to diagnose. Walk me through how you found the root cause.
Tell me about a time a performance regression appeared in production. How did you profile it, identify the bottleneck, and validate the fix?
How would you approach debugging a microservice that is intermittently returning 5xx errors in production with no obvious pattern?
Tell me about a significant technical architecture decision you made. What alternatives did you evaluate and why did you go with your choice?
Describe a time you had to refactor or migrate a legacy system while keeping it running in production. How did you plan and execute it safely?
How do you decide when to build something in-house versus using a third-party service or open-source library? Walk me through a real decision.
Walk me through your most recent role. What systems or features did you own and what was your most significant technical contribution?
Tell me about the project on your resume you are most proud of technically. What made it challenging and what was your specific contribution?
You list a specific technology or framework on your resume. Tell me about the most complex problem you solved using it.
Walk me through a time you had to learn a new language, framework, or domain quickly and still deliver. How did you ramp up and what did you build?

You need to build a REST API that lets users create and retrieve blog posts stored in a SQL database. Walk me through the endpoints you would design, the schema, and how you would handle basic validation.
Your Express server is returning a 500 error on POST /users. The error log says 'Cannot read properties of undefined'. How do you systematically find and fix the bug?
What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous request handling in a backend server, and when would you choose one over the other?
Design a paginated REST API for a product catalog with 10 million records. How do you handle sorting, filtering, and ensuring consistent pagination under concurrent writes?

A backend service that was handling 1,000 req/s is suddenly timing out under the same load after a deploy. Walk me through how you would diagnose whether the bottleneck is CPU, I/O, the database, or an external dependency.
Your monolith has a single PostgreSQL database and two teams want to own separate domains. How do you start decomposing into services without a big-bang rewrite?

Design a multi-tenant SaaS API where each tenant's data must be fully isolated. Compare row-level security, schema-per-tenant, and database-per-tenant — when would you choose each and what are the operational implications?

A microservice shows p99 latency of 2 seconds but p50 is 20ms. You have distributed traces, metrics, and logs. Walk through your investigation — what do those percentile patterns tell you and where do you look first?

You're migrating a payment processing service from synchronous request/response to an event-driven architecture. What consistency guarantees do you need to maintain, how do you handle exactly-once delivery, and how do you roll this out safely?
You're building a to-do app with a React frontend and a Node.js backend. Walk me through how data flows from the database to what the user sees on screen.
A user reports that clicking 'Save' on a form shows a success toast but the data is gone when they refresh. How do you debug this — where do you start and what tools do you use?
What are the trade-offs between storing user session state in a cookie, localStorage, or a server-side session store?
Design a real-time collaborative document editor (like Google Docs lite). Focus on how you synchronize state between clients and the server — what protocol and data model do you use?

Your Next.js app is fast on first load but slow on subsequent client-side navigations. Describe how you'd profile the issue using React DevTools, Lighthouse, and the network waterfall, and what common culprits you'd check.
For a content-heavy marketing site with a dynamic dashboard behind login, how do you decide which pages to SSR, SSG, or render client-side? Walk through the trade-offs.

Design a feature-flagging system that lets you roll out features to 1% of users with real-time targeting rules and zero-downtime flag updates. Consider how both the frontend and backend consume flags consistently.

A hydration mismatch error is appearing intermittently in production on your SSR Next.js app but never locally. Walk through how you would reproduce it, identify the root cause, and fix it without disabling SSR.

You need to support offline-first behavior in a web app — users should be able to make changes without a network connection and sync when back online. Design the sync protocol, conflict resolution strategy, and how you communicate sync state to the user.
You want to build a sentiment classifier for customer reviews. Walk me through the steps: data collection, model choice, evaluation, and deployment. What would you do if you had no labeled data?
Your model achieves 95% accuracy on your test set but performs poorly in production. What are three likely explanations and how would you investigate each?
What is the difference between a batch prediction pipeline and a real-time inference service? When would you use each?

Design a RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) system for a customer support chatbot that answers questions from a large internal knowledge base. Walk through the embedding pipeline, vector store, retrieval strategy, and how you evaluate answer quality.

Your LLM-powered feature generates correct answers in testing but starts hallucinating in production for certain user inputs. Describe your debugging approach — what data do you collect and how do you determine if it's a prompt, retrieval, or model issue?

You need to serve a 7B-parameter language model to 10,000 concurrent users with p95 latency under 500ms. Walk through your infrastructure decisions: quantization, batching, hardware choice, and auto-scaling.

Design an MLOps platform that supports model versioning, A/B testing between model versions, automated retraining when data drift is detected, and rollback with zero user impact. How do you handle the transition from offline evaluation to online metrics?

Your recommendation model's click-through rate has degraded by 15% over 6 months despite no code changes. Walk through how you diagnose between data drift, feature pipeline issues, label shift, and model staleness — and how you'd attribute the root cause quantitatively.

You're building a multi-agent system where specialized LLM agents collaborate to complete long-horizon tasks. How do you design the orchestration layer, handle partial failures, prevent infinite loops, and ensure the system remains observable and debuggable?
You need to host a static React app and a simple Node.js API on AWS. Walk me through the services you'd use, how you'd deploy them, and how you'd set up a custom domain with HTTPS.
Your Lambda function is timing out intermittently. Walk me through the CloudWatch logs and metrics you'd check first and what configuration you'd adjust.
What is the difference between a security group and a network ACL in AWS, and when is each the right tool?
Design a fault-tolerant file processing pipeline on AWS: files are uploaded to S3, processed asynchronously, and users are notified of results. Handle failures, retries, and dead-letter queues.

Your ECS service is healthy according to the load balancer but users report intermittent errors. Walk through how you would use CloudWatch, X-Ray, and ALB access logs to correlate the errors to a specific container or task.

Your team is spending 40% more on AWS than budgeted. Walk me through a structured cost optimization review — what services do you look at first, what tools do you use, and what changes typically have the highest ROI?

Design a multi-region active-active architecture for a payment service that needs 99.99% availability and sub-200ms latency globally. Address data replication, conflict resolution, traffic routing, and how you test regional failover.

During a traffic spike, your auto-scaling group is scaling out but requests are still timing out for 3 minutes after new instances launch. Walk through the possible causes in each layer — ALB, ASG, instance bootstrap, application warmup — and how you'd fix each.

Design an IAM strategy for an organization with 50 AWS accounts, multiple teams, and a requirement that no human has standing access to production. Cover account structure, permission boundaries, role federation, and how you audit access events.
Walk me through how React re-renders work. When does a component re-render and what can you do to avoid unnecessary re-renders?
A button's click handler is firing twice on every click. Walk me through how you'd reproduce it reliably, identify the cause in React, and fix it.
What is the difference between controlled and uncontrolled form inputs in React, and when would you prefer one over the other?

Design a client-side data table component that supports sorting, filtering, and pagination for up to 100,000 rows. How do you handle rendering performance, and at what point does virtualization become necessary?

Your React app's Largest Contentful Paint score has degraded from 1.2s to 3.8s after a recent release. Walk through how you'd use Chrome DevTools, Lighthouse, and bundle analysis to find and fix the regression.
Compare the trade-offs between keeping all state in a global store (Redux/Zustand) versus colocating state close to the components that use it. When does each approach break down?

Design a component library that supports white-labeling (custom themes per customer) and is consumed by five different product teams. Cover token architecture, build system, versioning, and how teams can override styles without forking.

Your app has a memory leak that only manifests in long user sessions. Walk through how you'd use Chrome DevTools heap snapshots and the Performance panel to identify what objects are not being garbage collected and trace them back to the source.

You're migrating a large React application from Create React App to a module federation architecture so three teams can deploy independently. Walk through the migration strategy, shared dependency management, runtime errors you'd expect, and how you test cross-team integration.
